records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Straitened (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Straitening.]

defs: To make strait; to make narrow; hence, to contract; to confine.
quotes: Waters, when straitened, as at the falls of bridges, give a roaring noise.
In narrow circuit, straitened by a foe.

defs: To make tense, or tight; to tighten.
quotes: They straiten at each end the cord.

defs: To restrict; to distress or embarrass in respect of means or conditions of life; -- used chiefly in the past participle; -- as, a man straitened in his circumstances.
